name: Bahrriya (Mother of Amr)
title: Epic-Making Mother
shortDescription: Junada's wife who, after her husband's martyrdom, sent her son to the battlefield and threw his severed head back at the enemy.
fullStory:
Bahrriya bint Mas'ud al-Khazraji was the wife of Junada ibn Ka'b and one of the very brave and pious women present in Karbala. Her husband, Junada, was martyred in the first attack on the morning of Ashura.

After her husband's martyrdom, Bahrriya prepared her young son (Amr ibn Junada), who was no more than 9 or 11 years old, and told him: 'My son! Go and help Imam Hussain.' Amr went to the battlefield and fought bravely until he was martyred.

Umar Sa'd's ruthless army severed the teenager's head and threw it towards the tents. Seeing her son's severed head, Bahrriya picked it up, wiped its blood, and said proudly: 'Well done, my son! O joy of my heart!'

Then, with Haidari bravery, she threw her son's severed head at one of the enemy soldiers, killing him. She then grabbed a tent pole and attacked the enemy with the intention to fight, but Imam Hussain (AS) returned her to the tents.
quoteArabic: أَحْسَنْتَ يا بُنَيَّ! يا قُرَّةَ عَيْنِي وَسُرُورَ قَلْبِي...
quote: (Upon seeing her son's head:) Well done, my son! O light of my eyes and joy of my heart!
